originalTitle: Fears for press freedom as billionaire takes control of East Africa's largest media house
title: A Very Rich Man Bought a Big News Company! What Does This Mean?
content:
Hello, Future Superstars!

Hello, amazing friends in Africa! I'm your kid-friendly news reporter, and I'm here to tell you about something important that happened in East Africa. East Africa is a part of our big, beautiful continent where countries like Kenya, Tanzania, and Uganda are found. It's a place full of sunshine, amazing animals, and wonderful people, just like you! Today, we're talking about news. You know, those stories you hear on TV, on the radio, or maybe even see in a newspaper? News helps us learn about our world. It tells us what's happening, what's new, and what's important.

What is News Anyway?

Imagine you just came back from school. Your mommy or daddy might ask, "What happened at school today?" When you tell them a story about your day, that's kind of like news! It's telling someone what really happened. News reporters are special people who find out what's happening in our towns, our countries, and all over the world. They talk to people, they visit places, and they write or tell stories for all of us to hear. They help us understand things like why prices at the market changed, or if there's a new school being built, or what our leaders are doing. **Did you know?** News can help you learn new things every single day! It helps you understand your community and the world around you.

A Very Big News Company

There is a very, very big company in East Africa. We call it a 'media house.' Think of it like a giant kitchen, but instead of making food, they make news! They have many busy reporters and writers and camera people. This big company makes newspapers, TV shows, and radio programs. They share news with millions and millions of people in East Africa. It's like the biggest news kitchen you can imagine! They help people wake up in the morning and know what's going on. This company is very important because so many people trust it to tell them the truth. It helps families know about their world. It helps kids like you learn too! **Did you know?** East Africa is home to amazing wildlife, like elephants, lions, and giraffes! Maybe some news stories talk about them!

Meet Rostam Aziz – A Very Rich Man!

Now, a very special person came along. His name is Rostam Aziz. Mr. Aziz is a billionaire! Can you say that? Bill-ion-aire! What does that mean? It means he has a lot, a lot, a LOT of money. Imagine you have a few coins. Your mommy or daddy might have some more. But a billionaire has more money than almost anyone you can think of! So much money, it's hard to count. Mr. Aziz decided to buy this very big news company. Yes, he bought the giant news kitchen! Now, he owns it. This is a very big deal, because when someone owns something, they get to make many of the big decisions about it. **Did you know?** A billion is a thousand million! That's a huge number. Just imagine how many candies you could buy with a billion shillings!

What is 'Press Freedom'?

This is a very important idea, even for little superstars like you. 'Press freedom' means that news reporters should be free to tell the truth. They should be able to report on what's happening, good or bad, without anyone telling them what to say or trying to stop them. Imagine you saw your friend share their toys fairly. You would tell your teacher that. But what if someone told you, "No, don't say that! Say they didn't share!" That wouldn't be fair, would it? Press freedom is like that. It means reporters can tell the real story, even if powerful people or rich people don't like the story. It's about being honest and fair. Why is this important? Because if reporters can't tell the truth, then we, the people, won't know what's really happening. We might only hear stories that make some people look good, even if it's not the full truth. **Did you know?** In many parts of the world, people work very hard to protect press freedom so that everyone can hear the truth.

Why Are People Talking About This Change?

So, Mr. Aziz, the very rich man, now owns the biggest news company in East Africa. This makes some people wonder. They are asking questions like, "Will the news still be fair?" and "Will the reporters still be free to tell all the truth?" When a very rich and powerful person owns a news company, there is a chance they might want the news to say things that are good for them. Or maybe they won't want the news to say things that are not good for them, even if it's true. This is called "journalistic independence." It means the reporters and writers are independent. They are free to do their job without their boss, or the owner, telling them what to say. They follow the facts, no matter what. People are a little worried that Mr. Aziz might tell the news company what stories to tell, or how to tell them. This could stop reporters from being truly independent. It could mean we don't always get the whole, true story. **Did you know?** Journalists are like detectives! They look for clues and facts to put together the real story.

What Happens Next?

So, what does this all mean for us? Well, we will have to watch and see. The reporters at this big news company will still try their best to find the truth and share it with everyone. It's important for all of us, even you, to keep listening to the news and to think. Always ask, "Is this true?" and "Does this sound fair?" Good reporters always try their best to be fair and honest. We hope that the news company will continue to tell stories that are true and important for all the people in East Africa. It's super important that everyone can get fair and honest news. **Did you know?** You can be a truth-teller too! Always tell your parents and teachers the truth, even if it's a little bit hard. Being honest is very brave!

Why is This Important for YOU?

Even though you are still little, this is important for your future. When you grow up, you will need good, honest news to help you make decisions, learn about your country, and understand the world. Good news helps our countries grow strong and fair. It makes sure that everyone has a voice and that important things are talked about. This change in East Africa reminds us how precious and important our news reporters and their freedom to tell the truth really are. So, let's keep learning, keep asking questions, and keep hoping for fair and true stories for everyone! **Did you know?** Africa is the second-largest continent in the world, full of diverse cultures, languages, and incredible history!
summary: A very rich man named Rostam Aziz bought East Africa's biggest news company, making some people wonder if the news will still be free and tell all the truth for everyone.
whyMatters: It's important because we need news reporters to be free to tell us what's really happening so we can all learn and make good choices, and not just hear stories that powerful people want us to hear.

originalTitle: France returns sacred 'talking drum' looted during colonial rule to Ivory Coast
title: France returns sacred 'talking drum' looted during colonial rule to Ivory Coast
content: The move forms part of a broader process of returning cultural artefacts to African countries that started in 2017.‎

originalTitle: Ghana star to deny rape charges, UK court hears
title: Our African Football Hero and Finding the Truth!
content:
Hello from Africa!

Hello, amazing kids! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you exciting stories from our wonderful continent, Africa! Today, we are going to talk about a very special football star from Ghana. His name is Thomas Partey, and he is a big hero to many boys and girls, just like you! Sometimes, even big heroes have a little bit of a puzzle to solve. We want to tell you about it in a simple way, focusing on fairness and finding the truth, which is super important for everyone!

Who is Thomas Partey?

Thomas Partey is a fantastic football player! He comes from Ghana, a beautiful country in West Africa. He plays in the middle of the field, running fast and helping his team score goals and stop the other team. He plays for our national team, the Black Stars of Ghana, and also for a famous football club in a country called England. Lots of kids in Africa dream of playing football like him! He is strong, talented, and works very hard. When he plays, he makes us all proud! Did you know? Ghana is a beautiful country in West Africa, famous for its delicious cocoa (which is used to make yummy chocolate!) and friendly people!

When Grown-Ups Have Problems

Sometimes, grown-ups, just like kids, have problems. It's like when you and your friend might disagree about something, or someone says you did something they don't like. A little while ago, a grown-up said something happened that was not very nice, and they talked about Thomas Partey. It's a bit like when someone says, "You took my crayon!" and you say, "No, I didn't, it's my crayon!" When big problems happen, we need to find out the truth.

What is a Court?

When grown-ups have big problems or disagreements like this, there is a special, important place called a 'court'. You can think of a court like a very serious school meeting, but for grown-ups and big problems. In a court, there are special grown-ups called 'judges' and 'lawyers'. Their job is to listen very, very carefully to everyone involved. They listen to what one person says happened, and they listen to what the other person says happened. They ask lots of questions. Their main goal is to find out the truth. They want to be fair to everyone and make sure everything is done correctly, just like a referee in a football game makes sure everyone plays by the rules. Did you know? A judge is a very clever grown-up in a court. They wear special robes and sit high up to help everyone follow the rules and find the truth!

Finding Out the Truth is Important

It is super important that everyone gets a chance to tell their story. Thomas Partey has said that he did not do anything wrong, and he wants to tell his side of the story. The court is where he will get to do that. The grown-ups in the court will listen to him and to the other grown-up very carefully. They want to know exactly what happened a little while ago, back in December 2020. It takes time to find the truth, because everyone needs to be heard fairly.

Rules Keep Us Safe and Fair

Just like in every game of football, there are rules in life. Rules help us live together, be kind to each other, and keep everyone safe. When someone says a rule was broken, it's very important to find out if it's true or not. This helps everyone feel safe and fair. We all want to live in a world where people are kind and respectful. When problems come up, we trust special grown-ups, like those in a court, to help us understand what happened and make things right.

What Happens Next?

Thomas Partey will go to the court soon. This will happen next month. He will talk to the grown-ups there and tell them his side of the story, just like we talked about. He has said that he will tell them that he did not do anything wrong. The court will listen to everything and everyone very carefully. After listening to everyone, they will make a decision. This is a process that grown-ups go through to make sure everything is fair for everyone involved.

Cheering for Fairness!

We love our football heroes, and we always want them to do well, both on the field and in life! When there are tricky problems like this, we hope for fairness and truth to shine through. It's a process that needs time and careful listening. We will keep cheering for Ghana and for all our heroes. We also cheer for truth and fairness because that makes our world a better place for everyone. Did you know? Football is the most popular sport in Africa! Kids everywhere love to kick a ball and dream of becoming a football star!

Let's Be Kind

Even when things are a little bit confusing or serious, it's always important to be kind and thoughtful. Let's remember that everyone deserves fairness and that finding the truth is a very important job for grown-ups. So, let's keep playing, keep learning, and keep cheering for a world where truth and kindness always win! We'll keep you updated if there's anything more to share in a simple way for our amazing kids.
summary: Ghanaian football star Thomas Partey will go to a special grown-up place called a court next month because someone said he did something wrong, and the court will help find out the truth fairly.
whyMatters: This story matters because it shows how grown-ups solve big problems in a fair way, by listening to everyone in a special place called a court to find out the truth, which is important for our heroes and for everyone to live safely.

originalTitle: 'Gruesome' war bets fuel calls for crackdown on prediction markets
title: Guessing Games and Kindness: A Special Story!
content:
Hello, My Friends!

Hello, everyone! It’s your friendly news reporter, always ready to share exciting stories from our amazing continent, Africa! I hope you are having a super day, playing and learning. Today, we have a very important story. It's about something we all do – making guesses! But it’s also about being kind and thoughtful. Let's learn together, shall we?

What is a Guess?

Do you ever guess things? Of course, you do! We all guess things every single day. Maybe you guess if it will rain tomorrow. Or maybe you guess what yummy food your grown-up will cook for dinner. Maybe you guess which animal is your friend’s favourite! A guess is when you try to think about what might happen. You don't know for sure, but you try to imagine. It's like a fun little puzzle in your mind! Sometimes your guess is right, and sometimes it's not. That's okay! It's just a guess. **Did you know?** Animals like birds can guess too! They guess where to find the best worms for breakfast!

What is Money?

Now, let's talk about money. Money is something we use to buy things we need and love. Like food, clothes, books, or even fun toys! Your grown-ups work hard to earn money. Then they use this money to buy nice things for you and for the family. We have different kinds of money in different countries. Here in my country, we might use Shillings or Rands. In other places, they use Dollars or Euros. But it all helps us buy things. **Did you know?** Long, long ago, before there was money like we know it, people sometimes traded things! Like, someone might give a goat for some yummy baskets of fruit!

Playing Games with Guesses and Money

Sometimes, people like to play games where they guess, and they put money on their guess. This is called a "bet" or "gambling." Imagine your favourite football team is playing. You might guess who will win. Some grown-ups like to put a little bit of money on their guess. If their team wins, they might get a little more money back! If their team loses, they don't get the money back. It's usually a fun game for grown-ups, like a puzzle with a small prize. But it's important to be careful with money and guessing games. Grown-ups need to be very smart about it. **Did you know?** The first games with guessing and money were played thousands of years ago! People have always loved trying to guess the future!

The Big News: Guessing About Sad Things Far Away

Now, here is the important part of our story. We learned about guesses, and we learned about money. And we learned that sometimes people play games with guesses and money. But what if people were guessing about something very, very sad? Something that hurts people? Far, far away from us, in a country called Iran, there have been some big, sad problems. When people in countries have very big disagreements and they fight, it can be called a "war." A war is not a nice thing. It makes many people sad, and it can hurt homes and families. And guess what? Some people were making guesses about these sad things happening in that far-away country. And they were putting lots of money on their guesses!

Why is Guessing About Sad Things Not Kind?

Imagine if your friend was feeling very sad because their toy broke. Would you guess if they would cry more, and then put money on that guess? No, you wouldn't! That would not be kind at all. You would want to help your friend, or make them feel better, right? It's the same thing when people guess about big, sad problems in the world. It’s not kind to make a game out of other people’s sadness or difficulties. When people make money by guessing about sad events, it feels wrong. It's like they are benefiting from someone else's trouble. It's much better to hope for good things and to be kind. **Did you know?** Kindness is like a super-power! When you are kind to someone, it makes both of you feel happy and strong!

What are "Prediction Markets"?

The grown-ups have a fancy name for these guessing places: "prediction markets." It sounds like a marketplace, doesn't it? Like where you go to buy fresh vegetables or fruits. But instead of fruits, people "trade" guesses. It's mostly on the internet. So, these "prediction markets" are like big online places where people can put money on what they think will happen next. They can guess about happy things, like who will win a big sports game. But they were also letting people guess about sad things, like the big problems in that far-away country, Iran. And people were putting lots and lots of money on these guesses. Not just a little bit, but like many, many colourful beads or shiny coins – millions of them! **Did you know?** The internet connects people all over the world, like a giant invisible web! You can learn about countries very far away, just like Iran, by using the internet with a grown-up.

Lots and Lots of Money!

Can you imagine millions of dollars? That's a super huge amount of money! It's like having a mountain of coins, or enough money to buy thousands and thousands of your favourite toys. So, people were putting this huge amount of money on their guesses about the sad problems in Iran. They were hoping to win even more money if their guess came true. This is why some important people got very worried. They said, "This is not right! We shouldn't be playing games with people's real-life sadness and problems!" **Did you know?** If you had one million US Dollars, you could build a brand new school for hundreds of children in Africa! That's how much money we're talking about!

Important People Say, "Stop This Game!"

Because this kind of guessing about sad things is not kind, many grown-ups who make important rules started to say, "Stop this game!" They said that these "prediction markets" should not allow people to put money on sad things like fighting or big problems between countries. They want to make sure that these online guessing places are used for fun, harmless things, and not for making money from other people's difficulties. They are working hard to make rules so this doesn't happen again. They want to protect people from using sad events as a way to win money. **Did you know?** There are special people called "leaders" or "lawmakers" who help make rules to keep everyone safe and fair in a country.

Let's Be Kind and Think of Others

So, what can we learn from this story, my dear friends? We learn that it’s fun to guess about many things, like the weather or games. But it’s never okay to guess about sad or hurtful things, especially when we want to make money from it. We should always try to be kind and thoughtful. We should think about how other people feel. When we hear about sad things happening far away, we should feel empathy. Empathy means understanding and sharing the feelings of others. We can send good thoughts, or if we are grown-ups, we can find ways to help people who are sad or in trouble. **Did you know?** When we help others, it makes our own hearts feel warm and happy, like sunshine!

Our African Heart of Kindness

Here in Africa, we know a lot about kindness and community. We believe in helping each other and sharing. We look out for our neighbours. This story reminds us that kindness should spread all over the world, not just in our villages and towns. Let's all promise to use our guesses for fun, and our hearts for kindness. Thank you for listening to this important story. Stay curious, stay kind, and remember to always think about others! See you next time for more news from Africa!
summary: Some grown-ups were using special online games to guess about sad problems happening in a far-away country, putting lots of money on their guesses, and now important people are saying this kind of game is not kind and should stop.
whyMatters: It matters because it teaches us that it's important to be kind and thoughtful, and that making games or money from other people's sadness is not right.

originalTitle: Record-breaker Dowman scores late as Arsenal beat Everton
title: Record-breaker Dowman scores late as Arsenal beat Everton
content: Sixteen-year-old Max Dowman becomes the youngest scorer in Premier League history, as Arsenal score twice in the last 10 minutes to beat Everton 2-0 and move 10 points clear at the top of the table.

originalTitle: Man City held by West Ham who move out of relegation zone
title: Man City held by West Ham who move out of relegation zone
content: West Ham come from behind to earn a point against Manchester City as Nuno Espirito Santo's side move out of the bottom three, while Pep Guardiola's side drop nine points behind leaders Arsenal in the Premier League.

originalTitle: Gordon goal gives Newcastle rare win at Chelsea
title: Gordon goal gives Newcastle rare win at Chelsea
content: Chelsea's hopes of qualifying for the Champions League are dealt a blow as Newcastle's Anthony Gordon scores the only goal of the game, to clinch a first away win at Stamford Bridge since 2012 for the Magpies.
